# DashMacro Userscript Authoring Spec (for LLMs)

You are writing a userscript for **DashMacro**, a Manifest V3 Chrome extension that runs
user scripts via the `chrome.userScripts` API. Paste this whole file as context when asked
to write a DashMacro script. Follow it exactly — DashMacro is NOT Tampermonkey and several
common assumptions are different.

## Metadata block (required-ish)

Start the script with a metadata block. Supported keys ONLY:

    // ==UserScript==
    // @name         My Script
    // @version      1.0
    // @match        https://example.com/*
    // @match        https://other.com/*
    // @exclude      https://example.com/logout*
    // @run-at       document-idle
    // ==/UserScript==

- `@name`     : shown in the DashMacro UI. First one wins.
- `@version`  : optional; used by "check for updates" when installed from a URL.
- `@match`    : Chrome match patterns. Multiple allowed. If NONE given, the script runs on
                EVERY site (`*://*/*`) — always add `@match` unless that is truly intended.
- `@include`  : treated as an alias of `@match` (same match-pattern syntax — NOT regex/glob).
- `@exclude` / `@exclude-match` : match patterns to exclude.
- `@run-at`   : one of `document-start`, `document-end`, `document-idle` (default `idle`).

IGNORED / UNSUPPORTED keys (do not rely on them): `@grant`, `@namespace`, `@require`,
`@resource`, `@connect`, `@icon`, `@downloadURL`, menu commands. Including them is harmless
(they're ignored) but they do nothing.

## Execution model — read carefully

- Scripts run in an **isolated world** (`USER_SCRIPT`), separate from the page's own JS.
  You CAN read/modify the DOM. You CANNOT read the page's own JS variables/globals
  (e.g. `window.somePageGlobal` set by the site is not visible). `unsafeWindow` is provided
  but equals the isolated `window`, so it does NOT give access to the page's real globals.
- **No external libraries.** `@require` is unsupported. Inline everything (no jQuery/CDN).
- Scripts inject **once per full page load** at `@run-at`. They do NOT re-run on SPA
  route changes. For single-page apps (React/Vue/Next), you MUST detect navigation yourself
  (see pattern below).
- `chrome.*` APIs are not available to script authors (only DashMacro's internal tracker
  uses messaging). Persist data with `GM_setValue`/`localStorage`.

## GM_* functions available

Auto-injected only if the source text contains `GM_` or `unsafeWindow`:

- `GM_getValue(key, default)` / `GM_setValue(key, value)` / `GM_deleteValue(key)` /
  `GM_listValues()` — JSON-serialized, backed by `localStorage`.
  IMPORTANT: storage is **per-site (per-origin)**, unlike Tampermonkey's cross-site store.
- `GM_addStyle(css)` — inject a <style>.
- `GM_log(...args)` — console.log with a `[GM]` prefix.

NOT available: `GM_xmlhttpRequest` (cross-origin blocked — use `fetch` for same-origin only),
`GM_registerMenuCommand`, `GM_notification`, `GM_setClipboard`, `GM_getResourceText`.

## Required patterns (LLMs get these wrong)

### 1. SPA navigation detection
If the target is a single-page app, hook history and re-run your logic on route change:

    function onRouteChange(fn) {
      const wrap = (type) => {
        const orig = history[type];
        history[type] = function () { const r = orig.apply(this, arguments);
          window.dispatchEvent(new Event('dm:nav')); return r; };
      };
      wrap('pushState'); wrap('replaceState');
      window.addEventListener('popstate', () => window.dispatchEvent(new Event('dm:nav')));
      window.addEventListener('dm:nav', () => setTimeout(fn, 500));
    }

### 2. Wait for an element (content loads async)

    async function waitFor(selector, timeoutMs = 8000) {
      const start = Date.now();
      while (Date.now() - start < timeoutMs) {
        const el = document.querySelector(selector);
        if (el) return el;
        await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 200));
      }
      return null;
    }

### 3. Setting value on a React/controlled input
Assigning `input.value = x` alone won't update React state. Use the native setter + events:

    function setNativeValue(input, value) {
      const setter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(
        window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set;
      setter.call(input, value);
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true }));
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }));
    }

### 4. Persisting state across full page navigations
Use `localStorage` (or `GM_setValue`). Anything in normal variables is lost on navigation.

## Minimal template

    // ==UserScript==
    // @name         Example
    // @version      1.0
    // @match        https://example.com/*
    // @run-at       document-idle
    // ==/UserScript==
    (function () {
      'use strict';
      // your code here
      console.log('DashMacro: Example running on', location.href);
    })();

## Checklist before returning a script
- [ ] Wrapped in an IIFE with `'use strict'`.
- [ ] Has `@match` scoped to the intended site(s) — not left blank.
- [ ] No `@require` / external scripts / CDN — everything inlined.
- [ ] For SPA sites: navigation is detected, elements are awaited, not assumed present.
- [ ] For controlled inputs: uses the native-setter + dispatch-event pattern.
- [ ] Does not assume access to the page's own JS globals.
